mission: Derive the minimal free-transport source abundance on the support branch and determine whether it can source the required Paper 35 baryogenesis target.

conditions: restrict to the admitted lowest-shell neutral even-CAR sector
require a gauge-neutral SO(3)-invariant additive scalar abundance observable
theorem_1_unique_minimal_abundance_obser.statement: Under the stated minimality conditions, the unique source-count observable is the centered total number operator on the active sub-sector. Its rms active count is N_act = sqrt(N p_H(1-p_H)).

theorem_2_free_transport_density_law.statement: Because the boundary seed covariance is fixed and radial transport is unitary and representation-preserving, the minimal active count is conserved under free transport. Therefore the free-transport source density on the support branch is n_free(R) = N_active / (2 pi^2 R^3).

theorem_3_free_source_yield_nogo.statement: On the minimal free-transport identification, the source yield at the exact crossing radius is Y_N,free. Since any free decay implementation can generate at most O(1) units of B-L per source quantum, the visible precursor obeys |Y_(B-L)| <= O(1) Y_N,free. The required target exceeds Y_N,free by the factor reported here. Therefore the minimal free-transport source is impossible as the Paper 35 baryogenesis source.

final_boundary.status: derived / sharpened exhausted Paper 35 source boundary
final_boundary.safe_statement: Under the minimal additive abundance identification and free support transport, the source yield is far too small. Therefore the support-side residual does not remain the pair (C_N, W_N) in practice: the free source is dead, so any positive closure requires a genuinely number-changing support interaction W_N. The coefficient side still reduces to the invariant selector (M1, m_tilde1, epsilon1).
remaining_objects: a theorem selecting the invariant coefficient data (M1, m_tilde1, epsilon1)
a genuinely number-changing support interaction theorem W_N
